body:
Fixes #479

## Summary
- Add the Electron Builder metadata required for Linux `.deb` output: homepage, author email, and Linux maintainer.
- Change `desktop-release-resolve.sh` so automatic desktop builds skip only when all 14 expected desktop assets already exist. Partial releases such as `v0.204.0` now rebuild instead of staying permanently missing Linux assets.
- Normalize Electron Builder's Linux x64 artifact aliases (`x86_64` AppImage, `amd64` `.deb`) to the `x64` filenames used by `/download`, checksum tests, and the release resolver before checksums/uploads.
- Preserve PR #487 investigation evidence in `docs/case-studies/issue-479`, including fresh issue comments, release inventory, Desktop Release run history, and downloaded CI logs.

## Reproduction / evidence
- Latest release `v0.204.0` had 10 uploaded assets and 0 `formal-ai-desktop-linux-*` assets (`docs/case-studies/issue-479/raw-data/latest-release-v0.204.0-assets.json`).
- Desktop Release run `27558290907` reached Linux packaging but failed `.deb` output because `desktop/package.json` lacked project homepage, `author.email`, and Linux maintainer metadata (`docs/case-studies/issue-479/ci-logs/desktop-release-27558290907.log`).
- The next Desktop Release run, `27563439476`, skipped because the old idempotency guard treated existing macOS/Windows assets as complete.
- Local Linux packaging after the metadata fix produced artifacts successfully; Electron Builder emitted `linux-x86_64` AppImage and `linux-amd64` `.deb`, and the new normalizer rewrote them to `linux-x64` names.

## Tests
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` (passed; warning-only pre-existing near-limit files)
- `git diff --cached --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose` (996 passed, 1 ignored, doc-tests passed)
- `cargo test --test unit ci_cd::desktop_release_resolve`
- `cargo test --test unit ci_cd::release_publishing`
- `cargo test --test unit ci_cd::workflow_release`
- `cargo build --release --bin formal-ai`
- `npm install --no-audit --no-fund --package-lock=false` from `desktop/` (local Node 20 warned; workflow uses Node 22)
- `node scripts/prepare-resources.mjs` from `desktop/`
- `CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY=false npx --no-install electron-builder --linux --publish never` from `desktop/`
- `node desktop/scripts/normalize-artifacts.mjs desktop/release`

<!-- hive-mind:working-session-summary -->
## Working session summary

PR 487 is updated, pushed, marked ready, and CI is passing: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/487

Implemented commit `37b8d085` on `issue-479-ff9c709d56dc`:
- Fixed the Linux `.deb` metadata failure by adding desktop `homepage`, `author.email`, and Linux `maintainer`.
- Fixed desktop release idempotency so a partial release with macOS/Windows assets no longer skips rebuilding missing Linux assets.
- Added artifact-name normalization so Electron Builder’s Linux `x86_64`/`amd64` outputs match the `/download` contract’s `linux-x64` names.
- Added regression tests and preserved investigation evidence/logs under `docs/case-studies/issue-479`.

Verified locally with focused CI tests, `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`, full `cargo test --all-features --verbose`, and local Linux Electron packaging. GitHub CI run `27565809374` is green, and the working tree is clean.
